consensus_issues.description: SHA-256 is not suitable for password hashing. It is too fast and lacks salting, making it vulnerable to brute-force and rainbow table attacks. Project rules require bcrypt.
auth_service is referenced but never defined or imported in the routes module. This will cause a NameError at runtime.
consensus_issues.suggestion: Inject AuthService via Flask's application context or a factory pattern.

openai.description: get_current_user returns None for invalid tokens, inconsistent with the exception-raising pattern in login.
openai.suggestion: Raise AuthError for invalid tokens or document the None-return contract.

anthropic.description: Session token is stored as plain text in the database.
anthropic.suggestion: Store a hash of the token and compare hashes on lookup.

gemini.description: The /me endpoint lacks a docstring, violating project rules.

summary.synthesis: All three providers identified the SHA-256 password hashing as a critical security flaw and the undefined auth_service as a runtime bug. Individual observations covered token storage, return value consistency, and missing docstrings.
